task: I’ve got a coworker who keeps asking if I can cover a few extra hours this week, and I’m trying to answer in a way that’s clear but not over the top. Can you help me word a short reply that says whether I can help out?

turns.agent_response:
Sure — keep it plain and direct.

### If you can help
**“I can cover a few extra hours this week. Let me know what you need.”**

### If you’re not sure yet
**“I might be able to help, but I need to check my schedule first.”**

### If you can’t
**“I can’t take extra hours this week.”**

### Slightly more natural
**“I can help out if it fits my schedule. Send me the details.”**

If you want, I can make it sound more like **text message**, **work chat**, or **in-person**.

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Plain enough. I’d probably use: “I can’t take extra hours this week” if I’m not able, or “I can cover a few extra hours this week” if I am. No need to make it fancy.
